title: P53-targeting vaccines and pd-1 pathway inhibitors and methods of use thereof
abstract: The disclosure provides, inter alia, methods and materials involved in treating cancer using a p53 vaccine in combination with a PD-1 pathway inhibitor.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is 1. A method of treating a cutaneous metastasis or a visceral metastasis in a subject in need thereof, the method comprising administering to the subject an effective amount of a p53-targeting modified vaccinia Ankara vaccine and an effective amount of a PD-1 pathway inhibitor to treat the cutaneous metastasis or the visceral metastasis. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the PD-1 pathway inhibitor comprises pembrolizumab, nivolumab, pidilizumab, avelumab, atezolizumab, durvalumab, BMS-936559, AMP -224, or a combination of two or more thereof. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the PD-1 pathway inhibitor comprises pembrolizumab. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the effective amount of the p53-targeting modified vaccinia Ankara vaccine is from about 2.3 x 10 8 pfu to about 3.3 x 10 8 pfu, and the effective amount of the pembrolizumab is from about 100 mg to about 300 mg. 5. The method of claim 3, wherein the effective amount of the p53-targeting modified vaccinia Ankara vaccine is from about 5.1 x 10 8 pfu to about 6.1 x 10 8 pfu, and the effective amount of the pembrolizumab is from about 100 mg to about 300 mg. 6. The method of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the subject is a breast cancer patient. 7. The method of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the subject is a triple negative breast cancer patient. 8. The method of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the subject is an estrogen receptor-expressing cancer subject, a human epidermal growth factor receptor 2-expressing cancer subject, or a combination thereof. 9. The method of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the subject is an indolent cancer patient. 10. The method of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein said subject does not have a detectable breast cancer tumor. 11. The method of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the subject is a p53- mutant cancer subject. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the p53-mutant cancer patient does not detectably express a mutated oncogenic protein in addition to a p53 mutant protein. 13. The method of any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein the method comprises treating the cutaneous metastasis. 14. A method for treating a metastatic cancer in a subject in need thereof, the method comprising administering to the subject an effective amount of a p53-targeting vaccine and an effective amount of a PD-1 pathway inhibitor to treat the metastatic cancer. 15. A method for treating cancer in a subj ect in need thereof, the method comprising administering to the subject an effective amount of a p53-targeting vaccine and an effective amount of a PD-1 pathway inhibitor to treat the cancer. 16. The method of claim 14 or 15, wherein the cancer is squamous cell carcinoma of the head and neck. 17. The method of claim 14 or 15, wherein the cancer is non-small cell lung cancer. 18. The method of claim 14 or 15, wherein the cancer is hepatocellular carcinoma. 19. The method of claim 14 or 15, wherein the cancer is renal cell carcinoma. 20. The method of claim 14 or 15, wherein the cancer is melanoma. 21. The method of claim 14 or 15, wherein the cancer is bladder cancer. 22. The method of claim 14 or 15, wherein the cancer is colon cancer. 23. The method of claim 14 or 15, wherein the cancer is rectal cancer. 24. The method of claim 25, wherein the breast cancer is triple negative breast cancer. 27. The method of claim 25 or 26, wherein the breast cancer is HER2/neu negative breast cancer. 28. The method of any one of claims 25 or 27, wherein the breast cancer is progesterone receptor negative breast cancer. 29. The method of claim 14 or 15, wherein the cancer is colorectal cancer. 30. The method of claim 29, wherein the colorectal cancer displays microsatellite instability. 31. The method of claim 14 or 15, wherein the cancer is pancreatic cancer. 32. The method of claim 14 or 15, wherein the cancer is ovarian cancer. 33. The method of claim 32, wherein the ovarian cancer is platinum-resistant ovarian cancer. 34. The method of claim 14 or 15, wherein the cancer is fallopian tube carcinoma. 35. The method of claim 14 or 15, wherein the cancer is peritoneal carcinoma. 36. The method of claim 14 or 15, wherein the cancer is a solid neoplasm. 37. The method of any one of claims 14 to 36, wherein the effective amount of the p53-targeting vaccine is a low dose amount of the p53-targeting vaccine. 38. The method of claim 37, wherein the low dose amount of the p53-targeting vaccine is a dose reduced by 5%, 10%, 20%, 30%, 40%, 50%, 60%, 70%, 80%, or 90% as compared 

 to a standard dose. 39. The method of any one of claims 14 to 38, wherein the p53-targeting vaccine is a modified vaccinia Ankara. 40. The method of any one of claims 14 to 39, wherein the PD-1 pathway inhibitor binds to a PD-1 receptor. 41. The method of any one of claims 14 to 40, wherein the PD-1 pathway inhibitor is a PD-1 antibody. 42. The method of claim 41, wherein the PD-1 antibody is a monoclonal antibody. 43. The method of claim 41 or 42, wherein the antibody is pembrolizumab, nivolumab, pidilizumab, atezolizumab, durvalumab, a biosimilar thereof, or a combination of two or more thereof. 44. The method of any one of claims 14 to 43, wherein the PD-1 pathway inhibitor potentiates anti-tumor activity of the p53-targeting vaccine. 45. The method of any one of claims 1 to 44, wherein the subject is a mammal. 46. The method of claim 45, wherein the mammal is a human. 47. The method of any one of claims 1 to 46, wherein the p53-targeting vaccine and PD-1 are administered sequentially. 48. The method of any one of claims 1 to 46, wherein the p53-targeting vaccine and PD-1 are administered concurrently. 49. The method of any one of claims 1 to 46, wherein the p53-targeting vaccine and PD-1 are admixed together prior to administration. 50. The method of any one of claims 1 to 49, wherein a response rate to the p53- targeting vaccine is reduced as compared to baseline reference or control reference. 51. The method of claim 50, wherein the baseline reference is obtained from the 

 subject prior to administration of the p53-targeting vaccine, PD-1 pathway inhibitor, or both. 52. The method of claim 50 or 51, wherein the control reference is obtained from a healthy subject, a cancer subject, or any population thereof. 53. The method of any one of claims 14 to 52, wherein the subject has been determined to have a p53 over expressing tumor prior to administration of the p53-targeting vaccine, and the PD-1 pathway inhibitor. 54. The method of any one of claims 1 to 55, wherein at least one dose of the p53-targeting vaccine is administered prior to at least one dose of the PD-1 pathway inhibitor. 57. The method of any one of claims 1 to 56, wherein the subject is administered 3 doses of the p53-targeting vaccine. 58. The method of any one of claims 1 to 57, wherein the subject is administered a maximum of 7 doses of the PD-1 pathway inhibitor. 59.
